nodes.context: Loose grouping of early-20th-century organized crime gangs formed among Italian immigrants from Naples and Campania in Brooklyn.
Neapolitan Camorra gang in Brooklyn headed by Alessandro Vollero and Leopoldo Lauritano.
Neapolitan Camorra gang under Pellegrino Morano operating from Santa Lucia restaurant in Coney Island.
Sicilian Mafia group based in Italian Harlem, Manhattan.
Modern organized crime groups that absorbed Neapolitan/Campanian groups after the Camorra's downfall.

edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets
edges.context: The two Camorra gangs cooperated against the Morello family.

edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets
edges.context: The Navy Street gang and Coney Island gang were in conflict with the Morello family.

edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets
edges.context: The Coney Island gang and Navy Street gang were in conflict with the Morello family.

edges.evidence_quote: Following the downfall of the New York Camorra, Neapolitan or Campanian organized crime groups in New York were absorbed into or merged with the newly dominant Sicilian Mafia groups in New York, creating the modern Italian-American Mafia
edges.context: The Brooklyn Camorra was absorbed into the Italian-American Mafia.
